categories.notes: Candidate stayed inside founder packet law and one-queue law, but uses more abstract ARC/CDR phrasing than the private canonical execution pack.
Candidate clearly separates TECHNICAL_PASS, FOUNDER_USABILITY_PASS, and POINT_B_COMPLETE and makes founder false block Point B closure.
Candidate preserves BP_PRIORITY as the only active queue and explicitly rejects false-green idle states.
Candidate is correct in blocked-return doctrine, but the canonical blueprint is more explicit step-by-step about what Builder/CDR may not decide during execution.
Candidate states the three-attempt doctrine, lessons carry-forward, research, and tier expansion more cleanly in one repair contract.
Reality-sync is stronger and clearer than the canonical artifact-sync wording because it binds receipt freshness to source hashes and deploy truth.
Founder-surface regression corpus is stronger than generic UI proof alone and directly captures the known founder-only failure mode.
Candidate did a better parts-car authority classification and caught amendment-era tag drift and dead proof alias drift explicitly.
Asset decision map is the strongest delta: it preserves good machinery and retires bad authority paths cleanly.
Candidate is strong conceptually, but the canonical pack is more executable because it includes a fuller end-to-end proof harness plan and more explicit implementation order.
Candidate requires same-tier determinism, but the canonical blueprint is stronger because it explicitly creates and runs determinism scripts as first-class steps.
Both packs keep FULLY_MACHINE_READY false until proof is real; candidate's certification lock clause is strong.
Transport-proof helper is sharper than the canonical build-proof wording because it names origin/main and deploy-status states more rigorously.
Candidate better distinguishes local success, remote transport, deploy sync, and live behavior truth.
Exact founder prompt corpus beats generic browser green and is the right answer to the Twisted Tea-style failure class.

architect_caught_that_canonical_missed: founder-surface regression corpus as a named durable artifact rather than only a proof run
dead-script proof alias risk around builderos:alpha:confirm
generated report freshness must bind to registry/BP/Point-B/deploy hashes, not only regeneration timing
already_present should downgrade to SKIPPED_ALREADY_VALID with proof instead of sounding like a code-changing PASS
active parts-car asset map with explicit REUSE/RETIRE/ARCHIVE classifications
canonical_caught_that_architect_missed: a more executable build order from BAC-000 through BAC-011
explicit creation of closure acceptance, founder UI proof, same-tier determinism, and build/deploy proof harness scripts
a stronger end-state path for certification update only after all proof receipts exist
promotion_decisions: Promote founder-surface regression corpus into the canonical blueprint.
Promote transport-proof vocabulary and explicit transport states into the canonical build-proof contract.
Promote reality-sync freshness fields and stale markers into the canonical artifact-sync plan.
Promote the asset decision map judgments into canonical salvage/authority artifacts.
Retain the canonical execution order and proof harness creation steps.

overall_verdict: Architect candidate is materially strong and found several real upgrades, but the private canonical blueprint remains the better execution blueprint once those upgrades are merged in.
promotion_actions: Merge the architect's founder-surface regression corpus requirement into the canonical blueprint and acceptance plan.
Merge the architect's transport-proof helper semantics into the canonical build-proof contract.
Merge the architect's reality-sync freshness/hash rules into the canonical artifact-sync contract.
Add a dead-script verifier for active npm proof aliases.
Preserve the canonical step-by-step harness creation and execution phases as the final implementation spine.
